mechanism_narrative: Aggressive and rapid increases in the federal funds rate signal a restrictive monetary policy that tightens financial conditions, increases the discount rate for future earnings, and compresses valuation multiples, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FOMC has hiked rates by 300bps from March to September 2022
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the trigger condition is met during a tightening cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A persistently low unemployment rate below its natural rate signals a late-cycle economy where wage pressures build, squeezing corporate profit margins and leading to lower forward returns as the economic cycle peaks.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.5% is near a 50-year low
failure_condition: A rolling 3-year correlation between the level of UNRATE and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive and statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real GDP signals deteriorating corporate earnings power and heightened risk aversion. This effect is amplified during periods of high inflation (stagflation), leading to strongly negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:US GDP contracted in both Q1 and Q2 2022
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL YoY is above 5%, quarters with negative GDP growth are not followed by statistically significant negative 20-day returns compared to positive growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Positive short-term momentum in industrial production reflects strengthening economic output and robust corporate revenue growth, which translates into positive S&P 500 returns over a very short horizon as earnings estimates are revised upwards.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between 3-month changes in INDPRO and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically positive over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of high inflation (CPIAUCSL) and low unemployment (UNRATE) forces the central bank into a restrictive policy stance to curb demand. This joint condition of an overheating economy and a hawkish Fed is strongly negative for equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:Current data shows UNRATE=3.5, CPI YoY is >8%
failure_condition: Periods where UNRATE < 4.0% and CPI YoY > 5.0% do not exhibit statistically lower 60-day forward returns than periods where only one or neither condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: A significant drop in housing starts from a recent peak indicates a rapid cooling of a key interest-rate sensitive sector, signaling a broader economic slowdown and lower expected corporate profits, leading to negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:30-year mortgage rates have more than doubled in 2022
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not statistically lower (t-test p > 0.05) in the 3 months following the trigger condition being met, compared to baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Strong month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates a healthy and expanding economy, which supports aggregate demand and corporate revenues, leading to positive short-term equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Payroll reports in mid-2022 remained resilient despite other slowing indicators
failure_condition: The rolling 3-year correlation between the 1-month percentage change in PAYEMS and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not consistently positive or turns negative.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of slowing nominal retail sales growth and high inflation implies a sharp contraction in real consumer spending. This erosion of consumer purchasing power is a strong leading indicator of recession and is negative for equity market returns.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year % change in RSAFS < Year-over-year % change in CPIAUCSL
evidence_refs: fact:Real retail sales have been negative year-over-year for several months in 2022
failure_condition: Periods where nominal retail sales growth is below the inflation rate are not followed by statistically significant negative 60-day S&P 500 returns compared to other periods.
